mnemonics: SCOPE CRASH: A project without good management will see its Scope, Cost, Resources, Approval, Schedule, CRASH.
keyPoints: A project is a temporary endeavor undertaken to create a unique product, service, or result.
Project management is the application of knowledge, skills, tools, and techniques to project activities to meet the project requirements.
The importance of PM lies in providing direction, mitigating risks, optimizing resources, ensuring quality, and delivering value.
commonMistakes: Confusing a project (temporary and unique) with ongoing operations (permanent and repetitive).
Assuming project management is only about creating a schedule (Gantt chart) and ignores scope, cost, quality, and risk.
